**Suisun City** ([/suːˈsuːn/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*soo-SOON*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"); [Patwin](/wiki/Patwin "Patwin") for "where the west wind blows") is a city in [Solano County, California](/wiki/Solano_County,_California "Solano County, California"), United States. The population was 29,518 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[8]](#cite_note-Census2020-8)

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Suisun_City,_California&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The city takes its name from the adjacent [Suisun Bay](/wiki/Suisun_Bay "Suisun Bay"), which in turn is named for the [Suisun people](/wiki/Suisun_people "Suisun people"), a [Native](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States") people of the area.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Suisun_City,_California&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Suisun City is located at [38°14′42″N 122°1′1″W﻿ / ﻿38.24500°N 122.01694°W﻿ / 38.24500; -122.01694](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Suisun_City,_California&params=38_14_42_N_122_1_1_W_type:city) (38.244863, -122.017048).[[10]](#cite_note-GR1-10)

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the city has a total area of 4.1 square miles (11 km2), of which 4.0 square miles (10 km2) is land and 0.06 square miles (0.16 km2) (1.48%) is water.

The city is adjacent to [Suisun Marsh](/wiki/Suisun_Marsh "Suisun Marsh"), at 84,000 acres (34,000 ha) the largest contiguous estuarine marsh remaining on the west coast of North America.

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Suisun_City,_California&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Climate")]

According to the [Köppen Climate Classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_Climate_Classification "Köppen Climate Classification") system, Suisun City has a [warm-summer Mediterranean climate](/wiki/Mediterranean_climate#Warm-summer_Mediterranean_climate "Mediterranean climate"), abbreviated "Csb" on climate maps.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### 2020

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Suisun_City,_California&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2020")]

The [2020 United States census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census") reported that Suisun City had a population of 29,518. The population density was 7,370.3 inhabitants per square mile (2,845.7/km2). The racial makeup of Suisun City was 28.5% [White](/wiki/White_Americans "White Americans"), 19.0% [African American](/wiki/African_Americans "African Americans"), 1.7% [Native American](/wiki/Native_Americans_in_the_United_States "Native Americans in the United States"), 19.8% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_Americans "Asian Americans"), 1.3% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_Americans "Pacific Islander Americans"), 13.4% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 16.4% from two or more races. [Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans") of any race were 29.1% of the population.[[13]](#cite_note-DP1-13)

The census reported that 99.8% of the population lived in households, 0.2% lived in non-institutionalized group quarters, and 0.1% were institutionalized.[[13]](#cite_note-DP1-13)

There were 9,316 households, out of which 39.6% included children under the age of 18, 51.9% were married-couple households, 7.2% were [cohabiting](/wiki/Cohabitation "Cohabitation") couple households, 25.9% had a female householder with no partner present, and 15.0% had a male householder with no partner present. 15.9% of households were one person, and 6.3% were one person aged 65 or older. The average household size was 3.16.[[13]](#cite_note-DP1-13) There were 7,283 [families](/wiki/Family_(U.S._Census) "Family (U.S. Census)") (78.2% of all households).[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The age distribution was 23.3% under the age of 18, 9.2% aged 18 to 24, 28.7% aged 25 to 44, 25.2% aged 45 to 64, and 13.6% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 36.7 years. For every 100 females, there were 94.8 males.[[13]](#cite_note-DP1-13)

There were 9,522 housing units at an average density of 2,377.5 units per square mile (918.0 units/km2), of which 9,316 (97.8%) were occupied. Of these, 67.5% were owner-occupied, and 32.5% were occupied by renters.[[13]](#cite_note-DP1-13)

In 2023, the US Census Bureau estimated that the median household income was $97,083, and the [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") was $37,217. About 6.4% of families and 8.7% of the population were below the poverty line.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### 2010

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Suisun_City,_California&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2010")]

The [2010 United States census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census")[[16]](#cite_note-16) reported that Suisun City had a population of 28,111. The population density was 6,752.3 inhabitants per square mile (2,607.1/km2). The racial makeup of Suisun City was 10,805 (38.4%) [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 5,713 (20.3%) [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 196 (0.7%) [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 5,348 (19.0%) [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 340 (1.2%) [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._Census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)"), 2,898 (10.3%) from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 2,811 (10.0%) from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 6,753 persons (24.0%).

The Census reported that 28,067 people (99.8% of the population) lived in households, 27 (0.1%) lived in non-institutionalized group quarters, and 17 (0.1%) were institutionalized.

There were 8,918 households, of which 4,013 (45.0%) had children under the age of 18 living in them, 4,856 (54.5%) were [opposite-sex married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 1,482 (16.6%) had a female householder with no husband present, 624 (7.0%) had a male householder with no wife present. There were 596 (6.7%) [unmarried opposite-sex partnerships](/wiki/POSSLQ "POSSLQ"), and 66 (0.7%) [same-sex married couples or partnerships](/wiki/Same-sex_partnerships "Same-sex partnerships"). 1,443 households (16.2%) were made up of individuals, and 350 (3.9%) had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 3.15. There were 6,962 [families](/wiki/Family_(U.S._Census) "Family (U.S. Census)") (78.1% of all households); the average family size was 3.52.

7,737 people (27.5%) were under the age of 18, 2,950 people (10.5%) aged 18 to 24, 7,850 people (27.9%) aged 25 to 44, 7,418 people (26.4%) aged 45 to 64, and 2,156 people (7.7%) who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 33.0 years. For every 100 females, there were 96.9 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 92.9 males.

There were 9,454 housing units at an average density of 2,270.9 units per square mile (876.8 units/km2), of which 6,184 (69.3%) were owner-occupied, and 2,734 (30.7%) were occupied by renters. The homeowner vacancy rate was 3.0%; the rental vacancy rate was 5.9%. 19,372 people (68.9% of the population) lived in owner-occupied housing units and 8,695 people (30.9%) lived in rental housing units.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Suisun_City,_California&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2f/American_flag_flies_in_front_of_Suisun_City_Train_Depot.jpg/250px-American_flag_flies_in_front_of_Suisun_City_Train_Depot.jpg)](/wiki/File:American_flag_flies_in_front_of_Suisun_City_Train_Depot.jpg)

American flag flies in front of Suisun City Train Depot

Suisun City was established in the 1850s. Its location made it ideal for commerce and transportation during the [California Gold Rush](/wiki/California_Gold_Rush "California Gold Rush").

In 1868—1869, it was connected to the [First transcontinental railroad](/wiki/First_transcontinental_railroad "First transcontinental railroad") at [Sacramento](/wiki/Sacramento,_California "Sacramento, California") via the [California Pacific Railroad](/wiki/California_Pacific_Railroad "California Pacific Railroad") (Cal-P) main line, expanding the region's reach across the United States. By 1879 [Central Pacific Railroad](/wiki/Central_Pacific_Railroad "Central Pacific Railroad") owned the Cal-P mainline and rerouted the transcontinental overland route through the new branch from Port Costa, via the [railroad ferry Solano](/wiki/Solano_(ferry) "Solano (ferry)") to [Benicia](/wiki/Benicia,_California "Benicia, California"), across the [Suisun Marsh](/wiki/Suisun_Marsh "Suisun Marsh") to Suisun City, putting the region directly on the overland route from San Francisco to Ogden and beyond.[[17]](#cite_note-alta30dec1879-17)

In 1888, a fire destroyed much of downtown Suisun, including many city landmarks, such as the Robert Hotel, Crowley's saloon, the Union Hotel, and the Orkel grain warehouses. Citizens considered abandoning the city after the devastation, but instead decided to rebuild and raise money to construct a waterworks system that would allow them to fight future fires more effectively.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

In the 1960s and 1970s, Suisun City experienced rapid growth as the San Francisco Bay Area's suburban ring expanded to the formerly rural Solano County. Also in the 1960s, [Interstate 80](/wiki/Interstate_80_in_California "Interstate 80 in California") was constructed two miles (3.2 km) outside the city, effectively moving commercial traffic away from railways and water conveyance.

In January 2025, the city of Suisun City announced that it was investigating expanding to lands to the east, effectively annexing much of [California Forever](/wiki/California_Forever "California Forever").[[19]](#cite_note-o243-19)
